title: Closure of certain minibus taxi routes in response to ongoing violence
description: NATIONAL NEWS - The Western Cape Government...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"NATIONAL NEWS - The Western Cape Government has confirmed that, from Wednesday, 17 September, several mini-bus taxi routes in the Cape Town metro will be closed for a period of 30 days in response to ongoing violence in the industry.","html":"NATIONAL NEWS - The Western Cape Government has confirmed that, from Wednesday, 17 September, several mini-bus taxi routes in the Cape Town metro will be closed for a period of 30 days in response to ongoing violence in the industry."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The closures are being implemented under Section 91 of the National Land Transport Act (2009) and apply specifically to routes and lanes in Mfuleni, Somerset West, Khayelitsha, Nomzamo, and Lwandle.","html":"The closures are being implemented under Section 91 of the National Land Transport Act (2009) and apply specifically to routes and lanes in Mfuleni, Somerset West, Khayelitsha, Nomzamo, and Lwandle."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"This measure is aimed at preventing further violence, ensuring commuter safety, and restoring stability."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"It is important to note that taxi ranks and roads will not be closed. Only the affected routes directly linked to the ongoing instability will be suspended.","html":"It is important to note that taxi ranks and roads will not be closed. Only the affected routes directly linked to the ongoing instability will be suspended."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"To ensure continued mobility for residents, Golden Arrow Bus Services (GABS) and Metrorail will provide alternative transport options during this period. Temporary permits may also be issued to substitute operators where needed.","html":"To ensure continued mobility for residents, Golden Arrow Bus Services (GABS) and Metrorail will provide alternative transport options during this period. Temporary permits may also be issued to substitute operators where needed."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"South African Police Service (SAPS), City of Cape Town Law Enforcement, and Provincial Traffic officers will be deployed to enforce the extraordinary measures. Operators who contravene the notice face fines of up to R5 000 or imprisonment of up to six months.","html":"South African Police Service (SAPS), City of Cape Town Law Enforcement, and Provincial Traffic officers will be deployed to enforce the extraordinary measures. Operators who contravene the notice face fines of up to R5 000 or imprisonment of up to six months."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Western Cape Mobility Minister Isaac Sileku acknowledged the gravity of the decision, “This was not an easy decision, but it is one that had to be taken to protect the lives of commuters and residents. The safety of our communities is our top priority. While extraordinary measures are in place, we will continue to engage with CATA and CODETA to work toward a sustainable resolution and long-term peace in the mini-bus taxi industry.”","html":"Western Cape Mobility Minister Isaac Sileku acknowledged the gravity of the decision, “This was not an easy decision, but it is one that had to be taken to protect the lives of commuters and residents. The safety of our communities is our top priority. While extraordinary measures are in place, we will continue to engage with CATA and CODETA to work toward a sustainable resolution and long-term peace in the mini-bus taxi industry.”"},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Closed routes"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The following routes are closed for 30 days from 17 September:","html":"The following routes are closed for 30 days from 17 September:"},{"type":"list","items":["611 Khayelitsha – Somerset West","43 Lwandle – Khayelitsha","M18 Mfuleni – Somerset West","X19 Nomzamo – Mfuleni","AA20 Khayelitsha – Somerset West CBD via Vergelegen Medical Clinic","AA21 Khayelitsha – Sitari Village Mall","R96 Khayelitsha – Somerset West via Somerset Mall","R97 Khayelitsha – Waterstone Mall – Somerset West","YEX63 Mfuleni – Somerset West","YEX64 Mfuleni – Strand via Somerset West"],"ordered":false},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Closed lanes of ranks","html":"Closed lanes of ranks "},{"type":"list","items":["Lane 1 - Khayelitsha (Nonkqubela & Makhaza)","Lane 1 - Mfuleni (new & old facilities)","Lane 2 - Somerset West Interchange","Lane 4 - Nomzamo taxi rank","Lane 4 - Lwandle taxi rank"],"ordered":false},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Western Cape Mobility Department remains committed to open dialogue with all stakeholders to secure a peaceful and sustainable resolution.","html":"The Western Cape Mobility Department remains committed to open dialogue with all stakeholders to secure a peaceful and sustainable resolution."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Closure of certain minibus taxi routes in response to ongoing violence
